title: Mandate
description: A principal's signed grant of bounded authority to a delegate (chp-v0.2.md section 10, proposal 0002): 'delegate D may invoke capabilities in SCOPE on my behalf until VALID_UNTIL'. The signature covers the canonical header {kind, mandate_id, delegate_id, scope, valid_from, valid_until, created_at, canonicalization}; the principal's host-identity attestation (with optional anchors) rides inside, so 'whose authority' resolves through the same trust roots as evidence bundles. A mandate narrows and attributes - transport auth still gates the connection.

delegate_id.description: The identity the principal is authorizing (the acting host/agent's caller name).

scope.description: Capability scope entries in the http-binding section-2 grammar: an exact capability id, or a trailing-* prefix (e.g. chp.adapters.audit.*). Sorted at signing time (canonical bytes).

valid_until.description: Mandates MUST expire - unbounded authority is what this object replaces.

host_identity.description: Self-signed host-identity attestation (chp-v0.2.md §3). 'anchors' is omitted entirely when empty (§3.1 byte rule).

anchors.description: External trust roots vouching for this key, e.g. {"type":"domain","domain":"acme.example"}. MUST be omitted when empty.

key_history.description: OPTIONAL rotation lineage (spec section 3.2): continuity statements, each signed by the OLD key it names. Omitted when empty.
